items.title: 'Less water than one restaurant': AI data centre company responds to concerns

children.content: One of three AI factories planned for northern Tasmania would be quieter than a library to the nearest resident, Firmus co-CEO Oliver Curtis says, as the company moves to assuage community concerns.

items.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on abuse victim payouts

children.content: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

items.title: Infants now eligible for free meningococcal B vaccine in Tasmania

children.content: Vaccinations for the rare but potentially deadly meningococcal B disease are now free for all Tasmanian infants for the next four years.

items.title: Boag's beer brand not for sale, but owner open to offers

children.content: The union representing Boag's brewery workers in Launceston wants its owner, Lion Australia, to sell the brand along with the brewery. But the company's response has left workers confused.
